description_long: This object is a compact, rectangular handheld electronic device with a prominent antenna extending from the top, suggesting it is a portable communication tool such as a walkie-talkie or two-way radio. The front face features a grid of physical buttons below a large orange display panel, with several horizontal slots above the display that likely serve as speaker or microphone grilles. The sides of the device are mostly plain, except for a vertical metallic strip, possibly a clip or battery compartment. The back of the device has a small protruding latch or clip, likely for attaching to a belt or strap, and a recessed area near the bottom. The overall color scheme is predominantly black with gray and orange accents, giving it a utilitarian and rugged appearance. The device appears to be constructed from durable plastic with some metallic components for structural support and functionality.
description: A rugged, handheld black communication device with an antenna, orange display, and a grid of physical buttons. Features metallic accents and a clip on the back.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the orange display, button grid, and speaker grilles.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the back clip, metallic side strip, and recessed area at the bottom.
description_view_2: Rear-right view focusing on the back clip and the plain side surface.
description_view_3: Front-right view displaying the orange display, button grid, and speaker grilles from a different angle.
